name: Anthropic Claude
description: This plugin provides integration between Flyte and Anthropic's Claude API, enabling you to use Flyte tasks as tools for Claude agents.

name: Apache Airflow
description: Airflow plugin allows you to seamlessly run Airflow tasks in the Flyte workflow without changing any code.

modules.name: AirflowConnector
modules.description: It is used to run Airflow tasks.

modules.name: AirflowObj
modules.description: This class is used to store the Airflow task configuration.

modules.name: AirflowTask
modules.description: This class is used to resolve an Airflow task.

name: Async FSSpec
description: The Flyte async fsspec plugin is a powerful addition to the Flyte ecosystem designed to optimize the performance of object transmission. This plugin focuses on overriding key methods of the file systems in fsspec to introduce efficiency improvements, resulting in accelerated data transfers between Flyte workflows and object storage.

name: AWS Athena
description: Flyte backend can be connected with Athena. Once enabled, it allows you to query AWS Athena service (Presto + ANSI SQL Support) and retrieve typed schema (optionally).

modules.name: AthenaConfig
modules.description: AthenaConfig should be used to configure a Athena Task.

modules.name: AthenaTask
modules.description: This is the simplest form of a Athena Task, that can be used even for tasks that do not produce any output.

name: AWS Batch
description: Flyte backend can be connected with AWS batch. Once enabled, it allows you to run flyte task on AWS batch service

modules.name: AWSBatchConfig

name: AWS SageMaker
description: The plugin currently features a SageMaker deployment connector.

modules.name: BotoConnector
modules.description: A general purpose boto3 connector that can be used to call any boto3 method.

name: Bigquery
description: BigQuery enables us to build data-intensive applications without operational burden. Flyte backend can be connected with the BigQuery service. Once enabled, it can allow you to query a BigQuery table.

modules.name: BigQueryConfig

modules.name: BigQueryTask
modules.description: This is the simplest form of a BigQuery Task, that can be used even for tasks that do not produce any output.

name: BigQuery
description: This plugin provides BigQuery integration for Flyte, enabling you to run BigQuery queries as Flyte tasks.

name: Comet ML
description: Comet’s machine learning platform integrates with your existing infrastructure and tools so you can manage, visualize, and optimize models—from training runs to production monitoring. This plugin integrates Flyte with Comet.ml by configuring links between the two platforms.

name: Dask
description: Flyte can execute dask jobs natively on a Kubernetes Cluster, which manages the virtual dask cluster's lifecycle

description: Run analytical workloads with ease using DuckDB.

name: Envd

name: Flyte Interactive
description: FlyteInteractive plugin provides users' favorite interface to develop and debug a flyte task interactively. We support vscode, jupyter (WIP), and neovim (WIP).

description: This plugin provides an implementation of the data persistence layer in Flytekit that uses fsspec. Once this plugin

name: Geopandas

name: Google Gemini
description: This plugin provides integration between Flyte and Google's Gemini API, enabling you to use Flyte tasks as tools for Gemini agents.

name: Google IAP
description: GCP Identity Aware Proxy (IAP) is a managed Google Cloud Platform (GCP) service that makes it easy to protect applications deployed on GCP by verifying user identity and using context to determine whether a user should be granted access. Because requests to applications protected with IAP first have to pass IAP before they can reach the protected backends, IAP provides a convenient way to implement a zero-trust access model.

name: Kubernetes Pod
description: By default, Flyte tasks decorated with @task are essentially single functions that are loaded in one container. But often, there is a need to run a job with more than one container.

modules.name: Pod
modules.description: Pod is a platform-wide configuration that uses pod templates.

name: Kubernetes Stateful
description: This plugin provides support for Kubernetes StatefulSet and Service integration, enabling seamless provisioning and coordination with any Kubernetes services or Flyte tasks. It is especially suited for deep learning use cases at scale, where distributed and parallelized data loading and caching across nodes are required.

modules.name: DataServiceConfig
modules.description: DataServiceConfig should be used to configure a DataServiceTask.

name: LLM-powered code generation and evaluation
description: Generate code from natural language prompts and validate it by running tests in an isolated sandbox. Works with any model that supports structured outputs (GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, etc. via LiteLLM) or directly with the Agent SDK (Claude-only).

modules.name: AutoCoderAgent
modules.description: Agent for single-file Python code generation with automatic testing and iteration.

name: Memory Machine Cloud
description: Flyte Connector plugin to allow executing Flyte tasks using MemVerge Memory Machine Cloud.

modules.name: MMCloudConnector
modules.description: Backend connector for Memory Machine Cloud.

name: Memray Profiling
description: Memray tracks and reports memory allocations, both in python code and in compiled extension modules.

name: MLflow
description: MLflow enables us to log parameters, code, and results in machine learning experiments and compare them using an interactive UI.

name: MLflow tracking

name: Modin
description: Modin is a pandas-accelerator that helps handle large datasets. It is a light-weight extension that is similar to the pandas API. It uses the concept of parallelism to reduce overhead, and improve the performance of pandas operations by leveraging the compute resources available.

name: Neptune
description: Neptune is the MLOps stack component for experiment tracking. It offers a single place to log, compare, store, and collaborate on experiments and models. This plugin integrates Flyte with Neptune by configuring links between the two platforms.

description: Flytekit python natively supports serialization of many data types for exchanging information between tasks.

name: Optuna (wrapper)
description: This documentation provides a guide to a fully parallelized Flyte plugin for Optuna. This wrapper leverages Flyte's scalable and distributed workflow orchestration capabilities to parallelize Optuna's hyperparameter optimization across multiple trials efficiently.

name: pandera
description: flyteplugins-pandera adds support for pandera.typing.pandas.DataFrame, pandera.typing.polars.DataFrame / LazyFrame, and pandera.typing.pyspark_sql.DataFrame in Flyte v2.

modules.name: ValidationConfig
modules.description: Controls behavior for pandera validation failures.

name: Pandera
description: Flytekit python natively supports many data types, including a FlyteSchema type for type-annotating pandas DataFrames. The Flytekit Pandera plugin provides an alternative for defining DataFrame schemas by integrating with Pandera, a runtime data validation tool for pandas DataFrames.

name: Papermill
description: It is possible to run a Jupyter notebook as a Flyte task using Papermill. Papermill executes the notebook as a whole, so before using this plugin, it is essential to construct your notebook as recommended by Papermill.

modules.name: NotebookTask
modules.description: Simple Papermill based input output handling for a Python Jupyter notebook.

modules.name: record_outputs

name: polars
description: This plugin provides native support for Polars DataFrames and LazyFrames in Flyte, enabling efficient data processing with Polars' high-performance DataFrame library.

name: Polars
description: Polars is a blazingly fast DataFrames library implemented in Rust using Apache Arrow Columnar Format as memory model.

name: pytorch
description: Union can execute PyTorch distributed training jobs natively on a Kubernetes Cluster, which manages the lifecycle of worker pods, rendezvous coordination, spin-up, and tear down. It leverages the open-sourced TorchElastic (torch.distributed.elastic) launcher and the Kubeflow PyTorch Operator, enabling fault-tolerant and elastic training across multiple nodes.

name: Spark
description: Flyte can execute Spark jobs natively on a Kubernetes Cluster, which manages a virtual cluster’s lifecycle, spin-up, and tear down. It leverages the open-sourced Spark On K8s Operator and can be enabled without signing up for any service. This is like running a transient spark cluster — a type of cluster spun up for a specific Spark job and torn down after completion.
